feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Development
feed.subtitle: ABAP Development Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Bug in ABAP Language
entry.content:
I just discovered my first bug in the ABAP Programming language introduced with SAP NetWeaver 7.4.

Some of our customers are still on very old releases and the bug just came up when they upgraded their system.

Prior to SAP NetWeaver 7.4, you were able to do the following:

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <result> type USR02.
DATA: results type table of USR02.

append initial line to results assigning <result>.

select * from usr02 into corresponding fields of <result>.
  append initial line to results assigning <result>.
endselect.

This code does exactly what you would expect it to do on a release lower than NW 7.4 - It appends all entries of USR02 to the table RESULTS.

However, starting with NW 7.4 the following happens:

  1. An initial line is appended to RESULTS and <RESULT> is assigned. <RESULT> now points to the first row of RESULTS.
  2. <RESULT> is populated with the first row of USR02, therefore the first row of RESULTS now also contains the data of USR02.
  3. A new initial line is appended to RESULTS and <RESULT> is assigned. <RESULT> now points to the second row of RESULTS.
  4. A new row of USR02 is selected. <RESULT> does not get populated, however the first row of RESULTS gets changed. The second row of RESULTS remains empty.

...

This is clearly a bug.

My guess:

Prior to NW 7.4, a pointer was passed to the select, now, the field symbol is no longer handled as a pointer within the select, instead the address of the work area is passed to the select.

This is confusing behaviour, as the same field symbols used in INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF and between SELECT and ENDSELECT now point to different addresses. This should not happen.

I suggest, that either the compiler will error when having to compile this, or that the previous, correct behaviour is restored.

entry.title: GOS Attachments Migration from SAP ECC to S/4HANA and Storage in SharePoint
entry.content:
We are migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA. Currently, GOS attachments for FB03 are stored in SAP tables such as SOOD, SOFFPHIO, and SOFFCONT1.

Our requirement is to store all new GOS attachments directly in SharePoint instead of these SAP tables.

In addition, we would like to migrate existing GOS attachments from SAP to SharePoint while maintaining the attachment links and accessibility from FB03.

Has anyone implemented a similar solution? What is the recommended approach, tools, or SAP-supported integration for storing GOS attachments in SharePoint and migrating historical attachments?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

entry.title: Issue with Dynpro Screen Download/Upload Between Systems After Namespace Change
entry.content:
We migrated a custom SAP development from System A to System B and changed the namespace during the process.

Example:
/ABC/PROGRAM → YXYZ_PROGRAM

Most objects work fine after renaming. However, when we download a Dynpro/screen from System A and upload it into System B, we get a “screen overflow” error. Even after updating all obvious references to the new namespace, the screen still doesn’t work correctly.


Has anyone encountered screen overflow or Dynpro upload issues after a namespace change? Are there hidden dependencies in screens that don’t get updated automatically during download/upload?
We migrated a custom SAP development from System A to System B and changed the namespace during the process.

Example:
/ABC/PROGRAM → YXYZ_PROGRAM

Most objects work fine after renaming. However, when we download a Dynpro/screen from System A and upload it into System B, we get a “screen overflow” error. Even after updating all obvious references to the new namespace, the screen still doesn’t work correctly.

Has anyone encountered screen overflow or Dynpro upload issues after a namespace change? Are there hidden dependencies in screens that don’t get updated automatically during download/upload?

entry.title: RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN Does Not Search Text Elements / Text Symbols
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While analyzing a requirement to identify occurrences of a company name in custom developments, I noticed that the standard report RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN does not return results when the text is maintained in program Text Elements (Text Symbols).

Example:

- A company name exists in a Text Symbol of a custom program.
- Running RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN with the company name as the search string returns no results.
- The same text is found when reading the program text pool using READ TEXTPOOL.

My understanding is that RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN scans ABAP source code but does not search Text Pools (Text Symbols, Selection Texts, List Headers, etc.).

Questions:

1. Is this an expected limitation of RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN?
2. Is there any SAP standard tool that can search Text Elements across programs?
3. How do you typically search for a text maintained in Text Symbols across custom developments?

As a workaround, I created a utility report using READ TEXTPOOL to scan text symbols in custom programs and it successfully identifies the occurrences.

Looking forward to your insights.

Thanks,
Aravindhan Chandran

entry.title: Custom Fields Working in Create CR but Not in Change CR – Missing LT_MAPPINGS Entry in MAP_DATAX_2AP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created new custom fields in KNA1 and added them to the MDG BP data model (BP_CUSGEN). The structures were regenerated and SMT mapping was maintained. During a Change Request, the field values are correctly populated in ER_T_DATA_UPD and the corresponding DATAX indicators are also set in CL_USMD_DELTA_BUFFER=>CALC_DELTA within the USMD_DATAX structure.

However, while debugging the method MAP_DATAX_2API, I noticed that my custom fields are not present in the LT_MAPPINGS  internal table. As a result, the fields are not being transferred correctly, and the changes are not updated in KNA1 during the change scenario. The create scenario works as expected.

entry.title: OBD HU packing dimensions not upating in TM status tab
entry.content:
Hi ,

We have developed a program to update the Outbound Delivery (OBD) HU packing dimensions using the function modules HU_GET_HUS, HU_HEADER_UPDATE and HU_PACKING_UPDATE.

However, the updated dimensions are reflected only in the SAP GUI application and are not being updated or displayed correctly in the Fiori application

Click freight unit item, it goes to friori app

in the below screen shot not updated the dimensions. but it's updated via VL02N.

 

Thank with Regards,

Vallamuthu M.

entry.title: Error in ADT-Plugin 3.60 when creating a new Data Definition
entry.content:
I encounter a NullPointerException in ADT-Plugin 3.60 when trying to create a new Data Definition by right-clicking on a package node and selecting 'New Data Definition'.

The error occurs because adtObject is null if no ABAP object is selected, only a structural package node.

As a work-around it is possible to "right-click" on an existing Data Definition

entry.title: BADI or User exit for CO40 - psttr Planned Order Start Date
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am looking for a suitable BAdI or user exit for CO40 and CO41.

Requirement: Validate and restrict the Planned Order Start Date - PSTTR during processing.

Advance Thanks

entry.title: eclipse/ADT
entry.content: I have a question for everyone. When the ADT plug-in of eclipse2026.6 version performs the request activation, the ATC verification fails. The previous version will jump directly to the view page of ATC Problems, but this version will not jump but a pop-up box will prompt that the request is locked. What is the problem? Can it be the same as before?

entry.title: How to Trigger Formula-Based MIC Calculation Programmatically via BAPI in SAP QM (QE11 Automation)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am working on automating the QE11 inspection result recording process through BAPI in SAP QM, and I have encountered a challenge related to formula-based MIC calculation.

**Background:**
In QE11, we have an inspection operation with multiple characteristics (MICs). One specific MIC — let's call it MIC 0020 ("AC Total Yarn Fault") — is configured with a formula in QP03 that automatically calculates the sum of all recorded defect values. This calculation is triggered when the inspection lot is unlocked (put into process) and the result is locked after computation. The field is display-only in QE11, meaning it cannot be manually entered — SAP computes and populates it internally.

**What We Are Trying to Achieve:**
We are replicating this QE11 flow programmatically using the BAPI `BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS`. The defect recording and result mapping for other MICs are working correctly. However, for MIC 0020, since it is formula-driven and display-only, passing the value directly via the BAPI is not possible — SAP does not accept manual input for this characteristic.

**The Core Question:**
When the unlock/lock action is performed in QE11, SAP internally triggers a formula function module (likely from the `QEFC_*` or `QEFM_*` namespace) that computes the summed result and writes it back to the characteristic. We need to replicate this same backend calculation programmatically.

Specifically, we would like to know:
1. How do we identify the exact formula function module assigned to a formula-based MIC from QP03 configuration (table `QPMA`, field `FORMELNR`)?
2. What are the standard import/export parameters for such formula FMs, and how should they be called in ABAP after recording the other MIC results?
3. Does the formula FM write the computed value directly to inspection tables (`QAMR`/`QAMV`), or does it return the value for us to pass back via BAPI?

**System Details:**
- S/4HANA (QM Module)
- BAPI Used: `BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS`
- MIC Type: Formula-based, display-only, result = sum of all defect characteristics

Any guidance, sample code, or pointers to relevant SAP notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thankyou

entry.title: Issue in standard FM SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT
entry.content:
Issue in standard FM SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT. as per the standard FM, we have entries till 4096.   

  TABLES: marm.

  TYPE-POOLS:truxs.
  DATA:itab1 TYPE truxs_t_text_data.
  DATA:wa_itab1(4096) TYPE c

Now we are using the text values of about 9000 entries. Please suggest to provide any solution for this, how we can add this values upto 9000 entries.

entry.title: Clarification Required for Manual Activity in SAP Note 2387818
entry.content:
We are currently implementing SAP Note 2387818 in our ES1 system.

During the manual implementation, we need clarification on the recommended implementation approach.

Could you please confirm whether the required fields or changes should be implemented by directly modifying the standard SAP structure or object?

Alternatively, is it recommended to create and use an append structure or enhancement instead?

If direct modification is the intended approach, please confirm that this is the implementation expected by SAP Note 2387818.

If an append structure or enhancement is supported, could you please provide the recommended implementation procedure or best practice?

We would like to follow SAP's recommended approach to ensure compatibility with future upgrades and note implementations.

Your guidance on the correct implementation method would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: GET Parameter ID in Update-Function-Module
entry.content:
I have written a Report in which i I set a parameter ID (= SET PARAMETER-ID Z....).
After this i do an Batch Input (Call Transaction MODE "E") on Transaction CO02 (Change Production Order) and just presses "PRINT" and then "SAVE". Within UPDATE-TASK (CO_VB_ORDER_PRINT).
a Smartform is called. In this Smartform i GET the parameter ID which is unfortunately always empty.

Why is the Parameter ID empty and what would be the best solution?

entry.title: Complete Beginner Seeking Guidance to Become an SAP ABAP Developer
entry.content:
I'm a final-year engineering student and a complete beginner to SAP. I don't have any prior knowledge of SAP, but I'm highly motivated to learn and build a career as an SAP ABAP Developer.

 

I recently got access to the SAP Learning Hub, Student Edition, and I'm a bit confused about where to begin. There are many learning journeys and courses available, so I'd really appreciate some guidance.

 

Could you please suggest:

 

- The best learning path for a complete beginner who wants to become an SAP ABAP Developer.

- Which courses or learning journeys I should complete first.

- Any hands-on practice resources, projects, or tips that helped you when you started.

 

Thank you in advance for your guidance and support!

entry.title: Material Group has not been defined error when using BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE
entry.content:
Hi all,

I was trying to update the contents of my Sales Order using 2 different methods:
1. SE37 using BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE

2. VA42(When the contract is updated, a custom code is calling BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE in the background to update all the related sales orders)

But ended up with the same results:



My team and I were not experiencing this error before. Is it possible that the material used in the Sales Order has been changed specifically in configuration side? Because this happens with new and old/existing sales orders.

entry.title: Issue in activating ZDMO_RAP_GENERATOR in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
In Cloud ABAP RAP I have pulled the abapgit files from cloud-abap-rap/README.md at main · SAP-samples/cloud-abap-rap · GitHub.

However, when I try to activate the ZDMO_RAP_GENERATOR I have errors in activation.

Can someone provide a solution to activate the ZDMO_RAP_GENERATOR.

entry.title: How to make specific fields read-only in an Action Parameter Popup (Abstract Entity) - ABAP 8.16
entry.content:
​I am working in an ABAP Release 8.16 environment using the RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model).

​I have a requirement where a custom action button on the UI triggers a popup dialog. This popup uses a custom Abstract Entity to define its input parameter fields.

​My Requirement:

When the user clicks the action button and the custom abstract entity popup appears, only the "Quantity" field should be editable. Every other field in that structure needs to be read-only.

​Here is a simplified version of my Abstract Entity:

@EndUserText.label: 'Action Parameter Popup'

define abstract entity ZC_MyActionParameter

{

  @EndUserText.label: 'Material ID'

  MaterialID : matnr;

  

  @EndUserText.label: 'Desired Quantity'

  Quantity : kwmeng;

  

  @EndUserText.label: 'Plant'

  Plant : werks_d;

}

 

And here is how it's defined in my Behavior Definition (BDEF):

action MyCustomAction parameter ZC_MyActionParameter result [1] $self;

Currently tried creating bdef for abstract entity and make feild readonly but not working 

And try to use object readonly UI annotation but is not available in this release

entry.title: Regarding unexpected behavior in batch input for ME12
entry.content:
We are developing an add-on program for SAP S/4HANA.
We are using an add-on program for the mass maintenance of purchasing info records, but it is exhibiting strange behavior. I would appreciate your advice on the cause and potential solutions.

The screen operations were performed in the following order:

#1. Updated purchasing info record for vendor XXXX in ME12 (updated price) by manual.
#2. a few minutes later. Updated purchasing info record for vendor YYYY in ME12 (updated price) by manual
#3. a few minutes later.Attempted to update purchasing info records (prices) for multiple items for vendor ZZZZ using the mass maintenance add-on.

For the first data record in step #3, the following error appeared:
"Supplier differs from supplier XXXX from the info record"
(Message Class 06, Message No. 314)
This unexpected behavior resulted in an error involving a vendor that was not being used at all in step #3.
We have been unable to intentionally reproduce the issue (it seems to happen unpredictably).

I would appreciate your guidance regarding the cause and how to address this.
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: How to hide create invoice in J1IGSUBCON
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

The requirement is to disable or hide the Create Invoice button in J1IGSUBCON.

Advance Thanks,

entry.title: Restrict Project Creation in CJ20N to Project Templates Only and Prevent WBS Structure Changes After
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I have a business requirement in SAP Project System (PS), and I would appreciate your advice on the best standard solution or enhancement approach.

Requirement:

  1. Users should only be allowed to create new projects in CJ20N by copying from a predefined Project Template.
  2. Creating a project from scratch should not be allowed.
  3. After the project is copied from the template, users should be able to maintain project data (dates, responsible persons, etc.), but they should not be allowed to:
    • Add new WBS elements.
    • Delete existing WBS elements.
    • Change the project hierarchy/structure.

The objective is to ensure that every operational project follows the exact WBS structure defined in the project template.
